edges.description: Failed cholesterol egress leads directly to lysosomal cholesterol storage.
NPC1/NPC2 transport failure also leads to secondary glycosphingolipid accumulation.
edges.target: Glycosphingolipid and Ganglioside Accumulation in Brain

edges.description: Core cholesterol-trafficking failure is linked to defective cellular autophagy.
NPC1/NPC2 trafficking failure is modeled upstream of early lysosomal sphingosine storage.
Cholesterol trapped in lysosomes is unavailable for normal esterification in NPC fibroblasts.
Disturbed neuronal cholesterol handling is reflected by oxysterol biomarker changes.
Visceral cholesterol and lipid storage contribute to liver and spleen enlargement.
Infantile hepatic storage disease produces cholestatic liver involvement.
Neonatal cholestasis manifests clinically as prolonged jaundice.
Stored lipids in tissue macrophages produce foam cells and sea-blue histiocytes.
Ganglioside and glycosphingolipid storage contributes to selective neuronal vulnerability in the cerebellum.
edges.source: Glycosphingolipid and Ganglioside Accumulation in Brain

edges.description: Brain lipid storage drives the progressive neurologic syndrome in NPC.
edges.source: Glycosphingolipid and Ganglioside Accumulation in Brain

edges.description: Purkinje cell loss produces cerebellar gait and coordination impairment.
Sphingosine storage depletes lysosomal calcium stores and perturbs calcium homeostasis.
Altered lysosomal calcium homeostasis contributes to secondary cholesterol storage.
Altered lysosomal calcium homeostasis contributes to secondary sphingolipid and ganglioside storage.
edges.target: Glycosphingolipid and Ganglioside Accumulation in Brain

edges.description: Defective lysosomal autophagy contributes to neuronal dysfunction and disease progression.
Neurological dysfunction in NPC contributes directly to dysphagia and swallowing difficulty.
Progressive neurological dysfunction in NPC contributes directly to seizures.
Ongoing neurological dysfunction contributes to progressive dementia and mental deterioration in NPC.
Adult-onset NPC can present with psychiatric manifestations before or alongside neurologic decline.
Hearing impairment is a recurrent neurologic/sensory manifestation, but the proximal path in this graph is not separated.
Bulbar neurologic dysfunction contributes to voice impairment.
Bulbar dysfunction and dysphagia contribute to feeding problems.
Hyperphosphorylated tau aggregates into insoluble neurofibrillary tangles.
Plasma p-tau217 is modeled as a blood biomarker reflecting tau pathology, not as a causal downstream disease mechanism.
Tau pathology is modeled as one contributor to cognitive decline in NPC.

edges.target: Glycosphingolipid and Ganglioside Accumulation in Brain

nodes.description: Co-inducer of heat shock proteins, approved for use in combination with miglustat for NPC. Enhances cellular stress response and may improve protein folding and lysosomal function.

evidence.snippet: Treatment for the neurologic manifestations of NPC can include miglustat (approved in several countries but not currently FDA approved for standalone treatment of NPC), arimoclomol (approved for use in combination with miglustat), and levacetylleucine (standalone treatment approved by FDA).
HP:0004333 | Bone-marrow foam cells | Frequent (79-30%)
The neurological disorder consists mainly of cerebellar ataxia, dysarthria, dysphagia, and progressive dementia.
HP:0001251 | Ataxia | Frequent (79-30%)

nodes.description: Progressive loss of cerebellar Purkinje cells is a hallmark neuropathological feature of NPC. Purkinje cells are particularly vulnerable to lipid accumulation and are among the earliest neuronal populations to degenerate.

evidence.reference_title: Intrathecal 2-hydroxypropyl-β-cyclodextrin decreases neurological disease progression in Niemann-Pick disease, type C1: a non-randomised, open-label, phase 1-2 trial.
evidence.snippet: In preclinical testing, 2-hydroxypropyl-β-cyclodextrins (HPβCD) significantly delayed cerebellar Purkinje cell loss, slowed progression of neurological manifestations, and increased lifespan in mouse and cat models of NPC1.
HP:0100543 | Cognitive impairment | Frequent (79-30%)

nodes.description: Impaired autophagic handling is an additional downstream mechanism in NPC that likely contributes to cellular dysfunction and disease progression.

evidence.reference_title: [Research advances in diagnosis and therapy of Niemann-Pick disease type C].
evidence.snippet: Impaired cholesterol trafficking and unesterified cholesterol accumulation in the late endosomes and lysosomals, as a results of mutations in NPC1 or NPC2 genes, are initial for the disease, and defective cellular autophagy, defective lysosomal calcium homeostasis and oxidative stress may all play roles in the physiological processes.
The neurological disorder consists mainly of cerebellar ataxia, dysarthria, dysphagia, and progressive dementia.
HP:0001260 | Dysarthria | Frequent (79-30%)
The neurological disorder consists mainly of cerebellar ataxia, dysarthria, dysphagia, and progressive dementia.
Death from aspiration pneumonia usually occurs in the late second or third decade.
HP:0002015 | Dysphagia | Very frequent (99-80%)
HP:0001618 | Dysphonia | Frequent (79-30%)
Cataplexy, seizures and dystonia are other common features.
HP:0001332 | Dystonia | Frequent (79-30%)
HP:0011968 | Feeding difficulties | Frequent (79-30%)
HP:0001288 | Gait disturbance | Very frequent (99-80%)

evidence.reference_title: Recommendations for the diagnosis and management of Niemann-Pick disease type C: an update.
evidence.snippet: Characteristic neurological manifestations of NP-C include saccadic eye movement (SEM) abnormalities or vertical supranuclear gaze palsy (VSGP), cerebellar signs (ataxia, dystonia/dysmetria, dysarthria and dysphagia) and gelastic cataplexy.
HP:0002524 | Cataplexy | Occasional (29-5%)

nodes.description: While cholesterol accumulates in peripheral tissues, the brain prominently accumulates glycosphingolipids including gangliosides GM2 and GM3. This ganglioside storage disrupts endosomal transport and is a major driver of neurodegeneration in NPC.
nodes.id: Glycosphingolipid and Ganglioside Accumulation in Brain

evidence.snippet: NP-C is currently described as a cellular cholesterol trafficking defect but in the brain, the prominently stored lipids are gangliosides.

evidence.reference_title: Accumulation of glycosphingolipids in Niemann-Pick C disease disrupts endosomal transport.
evidence.snippet: High pressure liquid chromatography and mass spectrometric analysis of NPC1(-/-) mouse brain revealed large increases in GSL.
HP:0000365 | Hearing impairment | Frequent (79-30%)
HP:0002240 | Hepatomegaly | Very frequent (99-80%)
The initial presentation is predominantly visceral with hepatosplenomegaly, cholestatic jaundice, and (in some instances) pulmonary infiltrates.
HP:0001433 | Hepatosplenomegaly | Occasional (29-5%)

nodes.description: HPbCD given by lumbar intrathecal injection mobilizes cholesterol from lysosomes. Phase 1-2 trial showed slowed neurological disease progression compared to natural history.

evidence.reference_title: Intrathecal 2-hydroxypropyl-β-cyclodextrin decreases neurological disease progression in Niemann-Pick disease, type C1: a non-randomised, open-label, phase 1-2 trial.
evidence.snippet: Patients with NPC1 treated with intrathecal HPβCD had slowed disease progression with an acceptable safety profile.
HP:0000952 | Jaundice | Very frequent (99-80%)
prolonged unexplained neonatal jaundice or cholestasis

Treatment for the neurologic manifestations of NPC can include miglustat (approved in several countries but not currently FDA approved for standalone treatment of NPC), arimoclomol (approved for use in combination with miglustat), and levacetylleucine (standalone treatment approved by FDA).
HP:0003349 | Low cholesterol esterification rate | Very frequent (99-80%)

nodes.description: NPC1-mutant cells have significantly reduced lysosomal calcium stores. This calcium depletion downstream of sphingosine accumulation causes secondary accumulation of cholesterol, sphingomyelin, and glycosphingolipids, linking calcium dysregulation to the broad lipid storage phenotype.

evidence.reference_title: Niemann-Pick disease type C1 is a sphingosine storage disease that causes deregulation of lysosomal calcium.
evidence.snippet: NPC1-mutant cells have a large reduction in the acidic compartment calcium store compared to wild-type cells.

nodes.description: Unesterified cholesterol accumulates in late endosomes and lysosomes as a defining storage abnormality in NPC.

evidence.reference_title: Niemann-Pick C1 disease gene: homology to mediators of cholesterol homeostasis.
evidence.snippet: Niemann-Pick type C (NP-C) disease, a fatal neurovisceral disorder, is characterized by lysosomal accumulation of low density lipoprotein (LDL)-derived cholesterol.

nodes.description: Substrate reduction therapy approved in Europe and several other countries for treatment of neurological manifestations. Inhibits glucosylceramide synthase to reduce glycosphingolipid accumulation.

evidence.snippet: A first product, miglustat, has been granted marketing authorization in Europe and several other countries for specific treatment of the neurological manifestations.

evidence.reference_title: Recommendations for the diagnosis and management of Niemann-Pick disease type C: an update.
evidence.snippet: Niemann-Pick disease type C (NP-C) is a rare inherited neurovisceral disease caused by mutations in either the NPC1 (in 95% of cases) or the NPC2 gene (in around 5% of cases), which lead to impaired intracellular lipid trafficking and accumulation of cholesterol and glycosphingolipids in the brain and other tissues.

nodes.description: Mutations in NPC1 or NPC2 impair export of cholesterol from late endosomes and lysosomes.

evidence.reference_title: Pluripotent Stem Cells for Disease Modeling and Drug Discovery in Niemann-Pick Type C1.
evidence.snippet: Both NPC1 and NPC2 are proteins responsible for the exit of cholesterol from late endosomes and lysosomes (LE/LY).

evidence.reference_title: Niemann-Pick C1 disease gene: homology to mediators of cholesterol homeostasis.
evidence.snippet: Transfection of NP-C fibroblasts with wild-type NPC1 cDNA resulted in correction of their excessive lysosomal storage of LDL cholesterol, thereby defining the critical role of NPC1 in regulation of intracellular cholesterol trafficking.

evidence.reference_title: Recommendations for the diagnosis and management of Niemann-Pick disease type C: an update.
evidence.snippet: Niemann-Pick disease type C (NP-C) is a rare inherited neurovisceral disease caused by mutations in either the NPC1 (in 95% of cases) or the NPC2 gene (in around 5% of cases), which lead to impaired intracellular lipid trafficking and accumulation of cholesterol and glycosphingolipids in the brain and other tissues.
The initial presentation is predominantly visceral with hepatosplenomegaly, cholestatic jaundice, and (in some instances) pulmonary infiltrates.

nodes.description: Hyperphosphorylated tau aggregates into insoluble intraneuronal neurofibrillary tangles, a neuropathological lesion that NPC shares with Alzheimer disease.

evidence.reference_title: Plasma phosphorylated-tau217 is increased in Niemann-Pick disease type C.
evidence.snippet: Niemann-Pick disease type C and Alzheimer's disease are distinct neurodegenerative disorders that share the presence of neurofibrillary tangle pathology.

evidence.reference_title: Plasma phosphorylated-tau217 is increased in Niemann-Pick disease type C.
evidence.snippet: Niemann-Pick disease type C and Alzheimer's disease are distinct neurodegenerative disorders that share the presence of neurofibrillary tangle pathology.

evidence.reference_title: Intrathecal 2-hydroxypropyl-β-cyclodextrin decreases neurological disease progression in Niemann-Pick disease, type C1: a non-randomised, open-label, phase 1-2 trial.
evidence.snippet: Post-drug plasma 24(S)-HC area under the curve (AUC8-72) values, an indicator of neuronal cholesterol homoeostasis, were significantly higher than post-saline plasma 24(S)-HC AUC8-72 after doses of 900 mg (p=0·0063) and 1200 mg (p=0·0037).

evidence.reference_title: Plasma phosphorylated-tau217 is increased in Niemann-Pick disease type C.
evidence.snippet: plasma phosphorylated-tau217 was increased in Niemann-Pick disease type C compared with controls (2.52 ± 1.93 versus 1.02 ± 0.34 pg/mL, respectively, P < 0.001) and inversely correlated with age at disease onset
The neurological disorder consists mainly of cerebellar ataxia, dysarthria, dysphagia, and progressive dementia.

nodes.description: NPC neurological disease produces a broad syndrome of progressive brain dysfunction with bulbar, oculomotor, seizure, and cognitive manifestations.

evidence.snippet: Affected patients are mainly characterized by neurological dysfunction and liver damage.

evidence.reference_title: Recommendations for the diagnosis and management of Niemann-Pick disease type C: an update.
evidence.snippet: an increasing number of cases are being detected and diagnosed during adulthood based on late-onset neurological signs and psychiatric manifestations.
ataxia not unfrequently following initial psychiatric disturbances (adult form).

evidence.reference_title: Niemann-Pick disease type C1 is a sphingosine storage disease that causes deregulation of lysosomal calcium.
evidence.snippet: sphingosine, glycosphingolipids, sphingomyelin and cholesterol accumulate

nodes.description: Sphingosine accumulates in the acidic compartment of NPC1-mutant cells almost immediately upon NPC1 dysfunction, preceding cholesterol and other lipid storage. This sphingosine accumulation is proposed as an initiating factor in NPC pathogenesis that triggers secondary lipid storage.

evidence.reference_title: Niemann-Pick disease type C1 is a sphingosine storage disease that causes deregulation of lysosomal calcium.
evidence.snippet: Sphingosine storage is therefore an initiating factor in NPC1 disease pathogenesis that causes altered calcium homeostasis, leading to the secondary storage of sphingolipids and cholesterol.
HP:0001744 | Splenomegaly | Frequent (79-30%)
isolated unexplained splenomegaly

nodes.description: NPC neurons develop hyperphosphorylated tau protein, paralleling Alzheimer-like tau pathology. Plasma phosphorylated-tau217 is elevated in NPC and inversely correlates with age at disease onset, indicating active tau phosphorylation as a disease process.

evidence.reference_title: Plasma phosphorylated-tau217 is increased in Niemann-Pick disease type C.
evidence.snippet: plasma phosphorylated-tau217 was increased in Niemann-Pick disease type C compared with controls (2.52 ± 1.93 versus 1.02 ± 0.34 pg/mL, respectively, P < 0.001) and inversely correlated with age at disease onset
The primary laboratory diagnosis requires living skin fibroblasts to demonstrate accumulation of unesterified cholesterol in perinuclear vesicles (lysosomes) after staining with filipin.
The most characteristic sign is vertical supranuclear gaze palsy.
HP:0000511 | Vertical supranuclear gaze palsy | Very frequent (99-80%)
